title: Maytenus dryandri
taxonomy: Maytenus dryandri belongs to the family Celastraceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its fruit and flower structure. The genus Maytenus is a group of flowering plants, and dryandri is one of the many species within this group. The Celastraceae family is a group of plants commonly known as the bittersweet or wax tree family. Members of this family are often shrubs or small trees with small flowers and fruit.

intro: Maytenus dryandri, also known as Chilean mayten or quebracho colorado, is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Maytenus dryandri include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, including maytansine, a potent anticancer compound. In traditional medicine, Maytenus dryandri is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and extracts.
